purpose: Source-backed repository of model or family outcomes for the advisor. These records say what is known to work, what did not work or remains blocked, and which claims still require local benchmark evidence.
claim_policy: Do not treat a working route as a speedup claim.
Keep source-reported numbers attached to hardware, workload, versions, and quality gates.
Use local_reproduced only for artifacts generated by this skill or a local benchmark receipt.
For top-model coverage, license and download metadata are demand signals; they are not implementation proof.

coverage_target.selection: Top downloaded public Hugging Face model records at collection time, with license metadata retained rather than silently assuming OSS.

status_definitions.local_reproduced: A local test, benchmark, parity fixture, or skill validation gate reproduced the result.
status_definitions.source_backed_working: A maintained repo, official docs, model card, or pinned implementation shows the route or behavior works, but this repo has not reproduced it locally for the selected model.
status_definitions.source_reported_benchmark: A source reports numbers with enough setup detail to show a real outcome, but the numbers are not portable.
status_definitions.known_limit_or_gap: The corpus found an explicit limitation, unsupported family, missing fixture, or model class that should not be presented as a working route.
status_definitions.unknown: No reviewed outcome is available yet.

records.label: MLX-LM decoder route
records.summary: For common decoder LLM families, start with MLX-LM conversion, loading, generation, cache, and quantization before writing a bespoke port.
worked: Conversion and generation route for common Transformers decoder models.
Weight quantization path exists through the MLX-LM conversion stack.
Speculative generation exists when draft and target tokenizer/vocabulary constraints are satisfied.
did_not_work: A working MLX-LM route is not a portable speedup claim.
Custom remote-code architectures, unusual cache semantics, or tokenizer mismatches still need static intake and parity gates.
records.claim_boundary: Claim route support and source-backed method availability; claim speed only after local target-workload benchmark metadata exists.
records.next_validation: Run static intake, source logits/cache parity, and target workload benchmark before publishing a numeric improvement.

records.label: MLX-VLM multimodal route
records.summary: For Qwen-VL, LLaVA-style, Pixtral, Gemma vision, and related image-text models, use MLX-VLM before inventing a new multimodal serving path.
worked: Maintained MLX-VLM route for image-text generation and serving.
Vision feature cache and automatic prefix cache implementations exist in pinned source.
vLLM-MLX has source-reported repeated-media serving outcomes for specific setups.
did_not_work: Repeated-media cache numbers do not generalize to one-off image prompts.
Media hashes, processor parity, placeholder alignment, and tenant isolation remain required gates.
records.claim_boundary: Claim source-backed VLM route and cache candidates; keep source-reported speedups attached to repeated-input serving setups.
records.next_validation: Run processor tensor parity, text+media output parity, cache key tests, memory accounting, and repeated-input benchmark.

records.label: Whisper ASR route
records.summary: Whisper-family ASR has working Apple-platform routes, but the advisor should separate Core ML or example support from generic MLX optimization claims.
worked: Whisper source model family and Apple-platform ports are established enough to guide intake.
ASR runbook gates can be applied to feature extraction, decoder state, timestamps, WER/CER, and real-time factor.
did_not_work: Core ML or example support is not proof of an MLX speedup.
Timestamp, chunking, language, and streaming behavior still need task-quality gates.
records.claim_boundary: Claim a source-backed ASR route and quality gates, not a generic MLX performance win.
records.next_validation: Run feature parity, decoded text/timestamp checks, WER/CER, real-time factor, and memory benchmark for the target audio distribution.

records.label: Encoder and embedding gap
records.summary: Many top downloaded models are embedding, reranker, BERT, CLIP, or feature-extraction models; this skill has routing gates but not a broad proven MLX outcome matrix for them yet.
worked: Architecture detection and runbook routing exist for encoder and non-generative CV families.
Static source-format intake can identify formats, tensors, and unsupported operators without executing remote code.
did_not_work: Do not imply MLX-LM support for sentence-transformers, rerankers, BERT encoders, CLIP, or arbitrary feature extractors.
Pooling, preprocessing, pair scoring, top-k/ranking metrics, and task-specific quality still need fixtures.
records.claim_boundary: Show as a known coverage gap or validation backlog, not as a benchmark-required speed candidate.
records.next_validation: Add model-class fixtures for pooling, preprocessing, pair scoring, logits/features, and task metric parity before calling these covered.

records.label: Diffusion and flow partial routes
records.summary: Diffusion and flow models have MLX examples and contributor ports, but outcomes are model-specific and cannot be promoted to a top-model-wide route without scheduler and conditioning parity.
worked: MLX examples and contributor evidence provide useful implementation leads.
Block-weight streaming has pinned evidence for repeated-block memory pressure.
did_not_work: Schedulers, latent scaling, conditioning, VAE/text encoder boundaries, and sampling quality make generic speed claims unsafe.
Memory-saving ports can worsen wall time if reloading dominates.
records.claim_boundary: Show as model-specific partial route or benchmark-required memory pattern, not a universal diffusion speedup.
records.next_validation: Run scheduler, latent, conditioning, image/audio quality, peak memory, and wall-time gates per model.

records.label: Audio and TTS partial routes
records.summary: MLX-Audio and Qwen3-TTS evidence can guide TTS/audio candidates, but each model still needs duration, vocoder, reference-audio, and perceptual quality gates.
worked: MLX-Audio package and Qwen3-TTS documentation provide source-backed route evidence.
Audio runbooks already separate ASR, codec, vocoder, autoregressive audio, and flow TTS gates.
did_not_work: Text-generation speed heuristics do not transfer to audio latency, RTF, or quality.
Speaker/reference audio, vocoder, chunking, sample rate, and codec behavior must be tested per model.
records.claim_boundary: Claim source-backed route evidence for matching audio/TTS families; benchmark first-audio latency, RTF, and quality locally.
records.next_validation: Run audio frontend parity, duration/vocoder checks, perceptual or task metric, RTF, and peak-memory benchmark.

records.label: Time-series and structured gap
records.summary: Top downloaded Chronos and structured/ranking models should not inherit LLM optimization guidance; they need task-specific preprocessing and metric gates.
worked: The skill has a time-series runbook and static inspection scaffolding.
Backlog records the needed fixture expansion for tabular, ranking, and recommender variants.
did_not_work: No broad proven MLX outcome matrix exists for Chronos, tabular, cross-encoder ranking, or recommender families yet.
Forecast metrics, leakage checks, pair scoring, candidate ID semantics, and top-k metrics are required.
records.claim_boundary: Show as validation backlog, not as an optimization candidate.
records.next_validation: Add scaler, lag, mask, quantile, leakage, pair-score, NDCG/AUC/top-k, and forecast-error fixtures.
